comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Plan-review PASS (signed, --ref HEAD) on iteration 5. Reviews oscillated on spec-precision but converged once I proved model.profile.supports_tools==True on the ACTUAL AnthropicModel (not just FunctionModel) and fixed the usage-warning false-fire (excluded model_override; requests>0 guard). Implemented + committed 2e037c9fc. Cached construction-time capability check (model.profile.supports_tools, gated on model_override is None + tools), usage-plausibility WARNING (never blocks), + invariant tests (tools offered, usage_limits on run* not ctor, capability fail-fast/noop/cache). 9 tests + 106 affected green; lint+typecheck clean. Not closing until Verified +1.

compiled_state.description:
Encode the agent-build invariants that guard against silently-disabled safeguards as tests plus a one-time construction check — not per-call runtime blocking. Every mechanism below is a CONCRETE, verified pydantic-ai 1.107.0 signal.

## Why
Several dangerous silent failures are static properties of rebar's OWN agent construction, not per-call events: tools silently stripped when a hook returns None (#5177), tools silently ignored on a non-tool-calling provider (#6186), `usage_limits` silently ignored if set on the `Agent(...)` constructor instead of `run*` (#1987), and usage silently zeroed on some provider adapters (#5360). Asserting these per-call wastes work and risks false-blocking a healthy run — they belong in tests + a one-time check.

## What (concrete mechanisms)
- **Construction-time provider tool-calling check.** VERIFIED signal: a pydantic-ai model exposes `model.profile.supports_tools` (a bool). The check is gated on `self._model_override is None` (the SAME guard as the anthropic model construction) — so the `model_override` test double is NEVER checked, concretely and by construction (no `.profile`-inspection heuristic to exclude it). It applies where rebar constructs an explicit model OBJECT (the anthropic path via `_build_retrying_anthropic_model`, the primary path in practice). Guard: FOR A TOOL-USING op (`tools` non-empty) where `model` is a model OBJECT exposing the capability — read defensively as `supports = getattr(getattr(model, 'profile', None), 'supports_tools', None)` (so a missing/None profile is a safe skip, never an AttributeError) — assert that when `supports is False` it raises (a `True`/`None` value passes); else raise `LLMConfigError` (fail fast). It is a NO-OP for the string-model paths (OpenAI/Gemini pass a model STRING to `Agent`, which pydantic-ai builds + validates internally — rebar has no object to introspect there) and for `model_override` (a test double). So the check bounds the path rebar actually owns without false-positiving on provider-string paths. CACHE: a module-level `set()` `_TOOL_CAPABILITY_CHECKED` keyed by the resolved model string — the check runs ONCE per unique model per process (not per call), so a hot loop pays nothing.
- **Tests — tools actually offered.** rebar builds the agent's tools as `pai_tools.filesystem_tools(cfg.repo_path)` (callables `read_file`, `list_directory`, `search_files`) + `pai_tools.rebar_tools(cfg.repo_path, allow_comment=…)` (`show_ticket`, and `comment_ticket` when allowed). A test asserts these builders return callables whose `__name__`s are exactly the expected set (so a #5177 hook-returns-None regression that strips a tool is caught).
- **Tests — `usage_limits` on `run*`, not the constructor.** A spy on `Agent`/`run_sync` asserts (a) `run_sync` IS called with `usage_limits=`, and (b) the `Agent(...)` constructor kwargs do NOT include `usage_limits` (the #1987 mis-placement). Confirmed: `Agent.run_sync` accepts `usage_limits`; the constructor does not honor it.
- **Usage-plausibility telemetry WARNING (never a block).** After a successful REAL run (`self._model_override is None` — offline test doubles report zero usage and must never warn), if `_extract_usage` reports BOTH `input_tokens == 0` AND `output_tokens == 0` while `requests > 0` (a request was actually made), log a WARNING (`llm usage looks zeroed/implausible …`) — an observability signal for the #5360 zeroed-adapter cluster. It NEVER blocks a gate (usage is observability, not load-bearing); a genuinely tiny run is at worst a benign warning.

## Scope
- Tests + the construction-time capability check + the telemetry warning. NO per-call runtime blocking for these invariants.
- NOT in scope: changing the tool surface or provider adapters themselves; a per-call usage assertion.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test: `pai_tools.filesystem_tools`/`rebar_tools` offer exactly the expected tool names — fails if a tool is stripped (`test_expected_tools_offered`).
- [ ] Test: `run_sync` is called with `usage_limits=` and the `Agent(...)` constructor is NOT — fails on the #1987 mis-placement (`test_usage_limits_on_run_not_constructor`).
- [ ] Construction-time check: a model OBJECT with `profile.supports_tools == False` used for a tool-using op raises `LLMConfigError`; a string-model path is a NO-OP (test asserts no raise); the check is cached in a module-level `set()` `_TOOL_CAPABILITY_CHECKED` (invoked once per model string; `test_capability_check_cached` spies on the `model.profile` access — or a counter in the check helper — asserting a repeated same-model run reads the profile only once) — `test_capability_check_fails_fast`, `test_capability_check_noop_for_string_model`, `test_capability_check_cached`.
- [ ] Usage-plausibility emits a WARNING (asserted via caplog) on an all-zero-usage successful run and NEVER blocks (`test_usage_plausibility_warns_never_blocks`).
- [ ] `make test`/`make lint`/`make typecheck` green.

Files: `src/rebar/llm/runner.py` (the cached construction-time capability check + the usage-plausibility warning), `tests/unit/test_agent_build_invariants.py` (new).

## Verified library behavior (experiment log — pydantic-ai 1.107.0, offline)
ACTUAL captured output (no billable call):
```
FunctionModel.profile.supports_tools == True; AnthropicModel("claude-sonnet-4-6").profile.supports_tools == True (bool)
  -> VERIFIED on the ACTUAL AnthropicModel type rebar constructs (offline, no API call — just the model object)
model.profile also exposes: supports_json_schema_output, supports_json_object_output, supports_thinking, ...
Agent.__init__ has tools/toolsets: True         (tools offered via the constructor; introspectable)
Agent.run_sync accepts usage_limits: True       (#1987: usage_limits belongs on run*, NOT Agent(...))
rebar tool builders: pai_tools.filesystem_tools -> [read_file, list_directory, search_files];
                     pai_tools.rebar_tools -> [show_ticket, (comment_ticket when allow_comment)]
_extract_usage returns a dict with input_tokens/output_tokens (both 0 => the zeroed-adapter signal)
```
These are pydantic-ai LIBRARY symbols (`model.profile.supports_tools` etc.), not rebar symbols
(bug succinct-formable-kite: a code-grounding pass must not read repo-absence as non-existence).

file_impact.reason: cached construction-time tool-capability check + usage-plausibility warning
tools-offered, usage_limits placement, capability check + cache, usage-plausibility tests

compiled_state.title: Agent-build invariants as tests + one-time capability check
